* [[Moe Davis]], retired [[United States Air Force|U.S. Air Force]] colonel, former director of the Air Force Judiciary, former chief prosecutor of the [[Guantanamo military commission]] (2005–2007), and 2020 Democratic nominee for U.S. House, NC-11 (Democratic)<ref>{{Cite web |title="When I ran for Congress in 2020, the one thing Madison Cawthorn and I agreed on was marijuana legalization. @ChuckEdwards4NC is fighting to keep criminal penalties for marijuana, which I'm sure is what his owners told him to do, but not his constituents." |url=https://twitter.com/ColMorrisDavis/status/1699848187530354757?s=20 |access-date=2023-09-08 |website=X (formerly Twitter) |language=en}}</ref><ref name=":7">{{Cite web |title="The Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians vote to legalize recreational marijuana by a 2.5 to 1 margin as ⁦@ChuckEdwards4NC fights the will of his constituents to appease his out-of-state owners … I mean donors." |url=https://twitter.com/ColMorrisDavis/status/1700111696809869388 |access-date=2023-09-08 |website=X (formerly Twitter) |language=en}}</ref>

entry.title: Wilhelm Schmid (painter)
entry.summary:
Added selected works from Aargauer Kunsthaus.

← Previous revision Revision as of 14:16, 10 June 2026
Line 12: Line 12:
Schmid exhibited in [[Munich]] and Berlin in 1916 and 1917.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=Nussbaum |first=Patricia |date=2016 |title=Wilhelm Schmid |url=https://recherche.sik-isea.ch/de/sik:person-4023473:exp/in/sikisea/actor |access-date=10 June 2026 |website=SIKART Lexikon zur Kunst in der Schweiz |language=de}}</ref> In 1918, he married the singer Maria Metz.<ref name=":0" /> He was a co-founder of the [[Novembergruppe]],<ref name=":1" /> and was a member of the group from 1918 to 1922 and again from 1930 to 1933.<ref name=":0" /> He later gave up architecture.<ref name=":0" /> From 1924 to 1931, he had a studio in [[Paris]], spending summers mainly in [[Provence]].<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />
Schmid exhibited in [[Munich]] and Berlin in 1916 and 1917.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=Nussbaum |first=Patricia |date=2016 |title=Wilhelm Schmid |url=https://recherche.sik-isea.ch/de/sik:person-4023473:exp/in/sikisea/actor |access-date=10 June 2026 |website=SIKART Lexikon zur Kunst in der Schweiz |language=de}}</ref> In 1918, he married the singer Maria Metz.<ref name=":0" /> He was a co-founder of the [[Novembergruppe]],<ref name=":1" /> and was a member of the group from 1918 to 1922 and again from 1930 to 1933.<ref name=":0" /> He later gave up architecture.<ref name=":0" /> From 1924 to 1931, he had a studio in [[Paris]], spending summers mainly in [[Provence]].<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" />


After returning to Berlin and [[Potsdam]], Schmid came under increasing pressure as a so-called “[[Kulturbolschewismus|Kulturbolschewist]]”.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" /> In 1937, he was listed among artists labelled “degenerate”, and his works were removed from museums.<ref name=":2" /> In 1938, he left Germany with his [[Jews|Jewish]] wife and settled in [[Brè sopra Lugano]].<ref name=":1" /> He died there on 20 November 1971.<ref name=":0" />
After returning to Berlin and [[Potsdam]], Schmid came under increasing pressure as a so-called “[[Kulturbolschewismus|Kulturbolschewist]]”.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" /> In 1937, his art was declared degenerate.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":2" /> In 1938, he left Germany with his [[Jews|Jewish]] wife and settled in [[Brè sopra Lugano]].<ref name=":1" /> He died there on 20 November 1971.<ref name=":0" />


== Work ==
== Work ==
Line 20: Line 20:


His work also drew on [[Magical realism|Magic Realism]]. Around 1923 and 1924, he moved away from more ambitious subjects and placed greater emphasis on painting technique. During the late 1920s and early 1930s, he painted figure compositions with billiard players, jazz bands and waiters. His later work in Switzerland consisted mainly of [[Ticino]] scenes, [[Landscape|landscapes]] and [[Still life|still lifes]].<ref name=":1" />
His work also drew on [[Magical realism|Magic Realism]]. Around 1923 and 1924, he moved away from more ambitious subjects and placed greater emphasis on painting technique. During the late 1920s and early 1930s, he painted figure compositions with billiard players, jazz bands and waiters. His later work in Switzerland consisted mainly of [[Ticino]] scenes, [[Landscape|landscapes]] and [[Still life|still lifes]].<ref name=":1" />

== Selected works ==
Schmid’s 1920 painting ''Luna'', also known as ''Mona Luna'' or ''Frau Luna'', is held by the [[Aargauer Kunsthaus]]. Together with ''Gelbe Musikanten'' and ''Traum des Pierrots'', it caused a scandal while bringing Schmid wider attention at the Juryfreie Berliner Ausstellung in 1920.<ref name=":2" />

After Schmid was listed among artists labelled “degenerate” in 1937, his works were removed from museums. Some of his major works, including ''Luna'' and ''Madame Dubarry'', were brought to Switzerland.<ref name=":2" />


==References==
==References==

entry.title: Naseer Khan Khoso
entry.summary:
← Previous revision Revision as of 14:16, 10 June 2026
Line 2: Line 2:
{{Use dmy dates|date=October 2020}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=October 2020}}
'''Mir Naseer Khan Khoso''' was a Chhutani [[Khosa (tribe)|Khoso]] from Madadpur village<ref>{{cite web |title=Pakistan Elections 2008 Candidates |url=http://www.elections.com.pk/candidates.php?elecID=4&assemblyId=3&constId=&na_loc=&start=151 |website=Pakistan Elections |accessdate=2019-11-27 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110721200335/http://www.elections.com.pk/candidates.php?elecID=4&assemblyId=3&constId=&na_loc=&start=151 |archivedate=2011-07-21 |url-status=usurped}}</ref> in the [[Thul]] Sub Division of [[Jacobabad District]].<ref name="Pas">[http://www.pas.gov.pk/index.php/members/profile/en/9/35 Mir Naseer Khan Khoso]</ref> He was a well known [[politician]] and became a [[Member of Provincial Assembly]] (MPA) in 1997 for the first time and became Minister of [[Sindh]]. In 2002 he was elected as MPA for the second time. He had also been the advisor to chief minister and minister for food.
'''Mir Naseer Khan Khoso''' was a Chhutani [[Khosa (tribe)|Khoso]] from Madadpur village<ref>{{cite web |title=Pakistan Elections 2008 Candidates |url=http://www.elections.com.pk/candidates.php?elecID=4&assemblyId=3&constId=&na_loc=&start=151 |website=Pakistan Elections |accessdate=2019-11-27 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110721200335/http://www.elections.com.pk/candidates.php?elecID=4&assemblyId=3&constId=&na_loc=&start=151 |archivedate=2011-07-21 |url-status=usurped}}</ref> in the [[Thul]] Sub Division of [[Jacobabad District]].<ref name="Pas">[http://www.pas.gov.pk/index.php/members/profile/en/9/35 Mir Naseer Khan Khoso]</ref> He was a well known [[politician]] and became a [[Member of Provincial Assembly]] (MPA) in 1997 for the first time and became Minister of [[Sindh]]. In 2002 he was elected as MPA for the second time. He had also been the advisor to chief minister and minister for food.

Mir Naseer Khan Khoso was born into the Chhutani Khoso family of Madadpur village in the Thul subdivision of Jacobabad District, Sindh. He belonged to a prominent tribal and political family that played an active role in the social and political affairs of upper Sindh. Throughout his life, he remained closely associated with the people of Jacobabad and surrounding rural areas.

Khoso entered mainstream politics in Sindh and emerged as a significant political figure in Jacobabad District. In the 1997 general elections, he was elected as a Member of the Provincial Assembly of Sindh from Jacobabad. Following his election, he served as a provincial minister in the Government of Sindh. He won the election as an independent candidate, defeating candidates from major political parties.
In the 2002 general elections, Khoso was again elected as a Member of the Provincial Assembly from Jacobabad, representing the Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP). His second term further strengthened his position in regional politics and public affairs.
During his years in politics, he was known for his influence in local governance, tribal affairs, and development issues concerning Jacobabad District and surrounding areas. He remained active in provincial politics for many years and maintained strong ties with his constituency.



He has four sons, Abdul Hafeez, Mahfuz-ur-Rehman, Anees-ur-Rehman and Muhammad Sadiq
He has four sons, Abdul Hafeez, Mahfuz-ur-Rehman, Anees-ur-Rehman and Muhammad Sadiq

Mir Naseer Khan Khoso is remembered as a prominent political leader of Jacobabad who represented the district in the Provincial Assembly of Sindh on multiple occasions. His political career spanned several decades, during which he contributed to provincial politics and local governance in upper Sindh.


He died on 24 December 2020, and is buried in his native village Mir Nawab Khan Khoso located in Jacobabad.<ref name="Pas" />
He died on 24 December 2020, and is buried in his native village Mir Nawab Khan Khoso located in Jacobabad.<ref name="Pas" />

=== Russia ===
=== Russia ===
In March 2023, [[Roskomnadzor]] requested Archive of Our Own to delete 16 fan fiction works, claiming they contained "child pornography".<ref>{{Cite news |date=6 April 2023 |title=Роскомнадзор нашел детское порно в американских фанфиках по мотивам "Гарри Поттера" и "Звездных войн" |trans-title=Roskomnadzor found child porn in American fanfics on "Harry Potter" and "Star Wars" |url=https://www.vedomosti.ru/media/articles/2023/04/06/969718-roskomnadzor-nashel-detskoe-porno-v-amerikanskom-samizdate-po-motivam-garri-pottera-i-zvezdnih-voin |access-date=13 July 2023 |website=[[Ведомости]] |language=ru|last=Тюняева|first=Марина|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230713093715/https://www.vedomosti.ru/media/articles/2023/04/06/969718-roskomnadzor-nashel-detskoe-porno-v-amerikanskom-samizdate-po-motivam-garri-pottera-i-zvezdnih-voin|archive-date=13 July 2023}}</ref>{{better source needed|date=May 2025|reason=Needs a source not controlled by the Russian government}} The site was subsequently [[Internet censorship in Russia|blocked in Russia]] on 14 April 2023, after failing to comply with the request.{{citation needed|date=May 2025}}
In April 2023, [[Roskomnadzor|RKN]] requested Archive of Our Own to delete 16 fan fiction works, claiming they contained "child pornography".<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-04-06 |title=Media: Russian watchdog demands that popular fanfiction website Archive of Our Own delete pornographic stories featuring teenagers written in Russian |url=https://novayagazeta.eu/en/articles/2023/04/06/media-russian-watchdog-demands-that-popular-fanfiction-website-archive-of-our-own-delete-pornographic-stories-featuring-teenagers-written-in-russian-en-news |access-date=2026-06-10 |website=Novaya Gazeta Europe |language=en}}</ref> The site was subsequently [[Internet censorship in Russia|blocked in Russia]] later that same month, after failing to comply with the request.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Europe |first=Novaya Gazeta |date=2024-07-12 |title=Russia blocks top fanfiction platform in continued crackdown on ‘LGBT propaganda’ |url=https://novayagazeta.eu/en/articles/2024/07/12/russia-blocks-top-fanfiction-platform-in-continued-crackdown-on-lgbt-propaganda-en-news |access-date=2026-06-10 |website=Novaya Gazeta Europe |language=en}}</ref>

== Your submission at [[Wikipedia:Articles for creation|Articles for creation]]: [[Draft:Dinesh Nandgave|Dinesh Nandgave]] (June 10) ==
<div style="border: solid 1px #FCC; background-color: var(--background-color-warning-subtle, #fdf2d5); color: inherit; padding: 0.5em 1em; margin: 1.5em; width: 90%;">'''Draft declined'''{{pb}}[[File:AFC-Logo_Decline.svg|50px|left]]Thank you for contributing to Wikipedia. Your draft submission to [[Wikipedia:Articles for creation|Articles for creation]] has been reviewed but not accepted at this time.{{pb}}
<nowiki> </nowiki>'''Feedback'''{{pb}}The reviewer, Hurricane Wind and Fire, left the following feedback:
{{divbox|gray|3=This draft appears to contain text generated by a [[large language model]] (such as ChatGPT). You [[Wikipedia:NEWLLM|cannot use LLMs]] to generate article content.
LLM-generated pages with [[Wikipedia:G15|certain obvious signs of being machine generated]] may be deleted without notice.

These tools are prone to specific issues that violate our policies:
* ''[[Hallucination (artificial intelligence)|hallucinations]]'': they often invent false information and cite non-existent references.
* ''[[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|unencyclopedic tone]]'': they tend to be vague, promotional, or essay-like, rather than neutral and factual.
* ''[[Wikipedia:Copyright violations|copyright issues]]'': they may closely paraphrase existing text, leading to copyright violations.

Instead, only [[Wikipedia:Writing better articles#Information style and tone|summarize in your own words a range of independent, reliable, published sources]] that discuss the subject.

See [[Wikipedia:Large language models|the advice page on large language models]] for more information.|}}<!--
-->{{divbox|gray|3=This draft does not include any sources or inline citations. Wikipedia's [[Wikipedia:Verifiability|verifiability policy]] requires that all content be supported by [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources|reliable sources]]. You should also use inline citations (footnotes) to show which source supports which specific statement.
The draft requires multiple published [[Wikipedia:Secondary|secondary]] sources that:
* provide ''[[Wikipedia:Notability#significant coverage|significant coverage]]'': discuss the subject in detail, not just brief mentions or routine announcements;
* are ''[[Wikipedia:Reliable sources|reliable]]'': from reputable outlets with editorial oversight;
* are ''[[Wikipedia:Independent sources|independent]]'': not connected to the subject, such as press releases, the subject's own website, or sponsored content.

Please add references that meet ''all three'' of these criteria. If none exist, the subject is not yet suitable for Wikipedia.

You must place an inline citation directly after:
* quotations;
* any material whose verifiability is likely to be challenged or is contentious;
* any material about living people.

Learn how to create inline citations in the:
* ''Source Editor'': [[Help:Introduction to referencing with Wiki Markup/1|Introduction to referencing with Wiki Markup]].
* ''VisualEditor'': [[Help:Introduction to referencing with VisualEditor/1|Introduction to referencing with VisualEditor]].|}}<!--
--
-->

'''Next steps'''
* Edit [[Draft:Dinesh Nandgave]] to address the points above, making sure to publish any changes.
* When you are ready to resubmit your draft for review, click the '''Resubmit''' button.
* If you do not edit your draft in the next 6 months, it [[Wikipedia:Criteria for speedy deletion#G13. Abandoned drafts and Articles for creation submissions|may be deleted]].

'''Need help?'''
* For an overview of our policies and guidelines see [[Wikipedia:Everything you need to know|Everything you need to know]].
* You can ask for help at the <span class="plainlinks" >[//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wikipedia:WikiProject_Articles_for_creation/Help_desk/New_question&withJS=MediaWiki:AFCHD-wizard.js&page=Draft:Dinesh_Nandgave '''Articles for creation help desk''']</span>, or on the <span class="plainlinks" >[//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User_talk:Hurricane_Wind_and_Fire&action=edit&section=new&nosummary=1&preload=Template:AfC_decline/HD_preload&preloadparams%5B%5D=Draft:Dinesh_Nandgave '''reviewer's talk page''']</span>.

'''Scam warning'''
* If anyone asks you for money or payment to publish, protect, or restore a Wikipedia article or draft, it is a scam. [[Wikipedia:Scam warning|Learn about scams on Wikipedia]].
🌀[[User:Hurricane Wind and Fire|Hurricane Wind and Fire]], [[User:Hurricane Wind and Fire/Why did I decline your draft?|why did you decline my draft?]] [[User talk:Hurricane Wind and Fire|(talk)]] [[Special:Contribs/Hurricane Wind and Fire|(contribs)]]🔥 14:16, 10 June 2026 (UTC)</div><!--Template:AfC decline-->

entry.title: Sydney School (linguistics)
entry.summary:
← Previous revision Revision as of 14:16, 10 June 2026
Line 34: Line 34:


== Background ==
== Background ==
The Sydney School is a genre-based literacy pedagogy that began developing in August 1979 at the Working Conference on Language in Education. This conference, organised by [[Michael Halliday]], is noted by [[J.R. Martin|J. R. Martin]] as being the place at which ideas about genre analysis as a lens to observe the way students are taught to write in [[Primary school|primary]] and [[secondary school]] were formed.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Martin|first=J. R.|date=2000|title=Grammar Meets Genre: Reflections on the 'Sydney School'|journal=Arts: The Journal of the Sydney University Arts Association|volume=22|pages= 47}}</ref> The significant developments of the Sydney School throughout the 1980s were based upon the assessment of primary schools’ treatment of [[Genre fiction|genre]] in education, consisting of a removed approach that was focused primarily upon recount and observation-based writing.<ref>Rose, David, and J. R Martin. ''Learning To Write, Reading To Learn''. Sheffield: Equinox, 2012. p. 66.</ref><ref>Rose, David. (2019). “Writing as linguistic mastery: the development of genre-based literacy pedagogy.” In ''Handbook of Writing Development,'' edited by D. Myhill, D. Beard, M. Nystrand and J. Riley. (London: Sage): 1.</ref> It was not until 1995 when the NSW [[Board of Studies]] designed their new English [[Kindergarten|K]]-[[Year Six|6]] syllabus that the theories of the Sydney School began to make an impact on pedagogy.<ref>Rose, David. (2019). “Writing as linguistic mastery: the development of genre-based literacy pedagogy.” In ''Handbook of Writing Development,'' edited by D. Myhill, D. Beard, M. Nystrand and J. Riley. (London: Sage): 7.</ref><ref>Martin, J. R. (2000). "Grammar Meets Genre: Reflections on the 'Sydney School'". ''Arts: The Journal of the Sydney University Arts Association.'' '''22''': 48.</ref> The title 'Sydney School' was given to the body of research however became quickly outdated as the study began to extended nationally. By 2000 the Sydney School had firmly become attained international reach.<ref>Martin, J. R. (2000). "Grammar Meets Genre: Reflections on the 'Sydney School'". ''Arts: The Journal of the Sydney University Arts Association.'' '''22''': 49.</ref>
The Sydney School is a genre-based literacy pedagogy that began developing in August 1979 at the Working Conference on Language in Education. This conference, organised by [[Michael Halliday]], is noted by [[J.R. Martin|J. R. Martin]] as being the place at which ideas about genre analysis as a lens to observe the way students are taught to write in [[Primary school|primary]] and [[secondary school]] were formed.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Martin|first=J. R.|date=2000|title=Grammar Meets Genre: Reflections on the 'Sydney School'|journal=Arts: The Journal of the Sydney University Arts Association|volume=22|pages= 47}}</ref> The significant developments of the Sydney School throughout the 1980s were based upon the assessment of primary schools’ treatment of [[Genre fiction|genre]] in education, consisting of a removed approach that was focused primarily upon recount and observation-based writing.<ref>Rose, David, and J. R Martin. ''Learning To Write, Reading To Learn''. Sheffield: Equinox, 2012. p. 66.</ref><ref>Rose, David. (2019). “Writing as linguistic mastery: the development of genre-based literacy pedagogy.” In ''Handbook of Writing Development,'' edited by D. Myhill, D. Beard, M. Nystrand and J. Riley. (London: Sage): 1.</ref> It was not until 1995 when the NSW [[Board of Studies]] designed their new English [[Kindergarten|K]]-[[Year Six|6]] syllabus that the theories of the Sydney School began to make an impact on pedagogy.<ref>Rose, David. (2019). “Writing as linguistic mastery: the development of genre-based literacy pedagogy.” In ''Handbook of Writing Development,'' edited by D. Myhill, D. Beard, M. Nystrand and J. Riley. (London: Sage): 7.</ref><ref>Martin, J. R. (2000). "Grammar Meets Genre: Reflections on the 'Sydney School'". ''Arts: The Journal of the Sydney University Arts Association.'' '''22''': 48.</ref> The title 'Sydney School' was given to the body of research however became quickly outdated as the study began to extend nationally. By 2000 the Sydney School had firmly become attained international reach.<ref>Martin, J. R. (2000). "Grammar Meets Genre: Reflections on the 'Sydney School'". ''Arts: The Journal of the Sydney University Arts Association.'' '''22''': 49.</ref>

entry.title: User talk:Nandgavedinesh44
entry.summary:
Welcome to Wikipedia!

New page



==Welcome!==
Hello, Nandgavedinesh44, and '''welcome to Wikipedia'''! Thank you for [[Special:Contributions/Nandgavedinesh44|your contributions]].

I noticed that one of the first articles you created or edited was [[:Draft:Dinesh Nandgave]], which appears to be a draft of an article [[Wikipedia:Autobiography|about yourself]]. Writing about yourself is a [[Wikipedia:Avoiding common mistakes|common mistake]] made by new [[Wikipedia:Wikipedians|Wikipedians]].

As this is an encyclopedia, we wouldn't expect to have an article about every contributor. We require individuals to meet Wikipedia's definition of a [[WP:BIO|notable person]] to accept articles about them. A page you created about yourself may well be deleted from the encyclopedia. If it is deleted and you wish to retrieve its contents, don't hesitate to ask [[:Category:Wikipedia administrators willing to provide copies of deleted articles|anyone from this list]] and they will copy it to your user page.

Here are some pages that you might find helpful:
* [[Help:Getting started|Getting started]]
* [[Help:Introduction|Introductory tutorial]]
* [[Wikipedia:Contributing to Wikipedia|Contributing to Wikipedia]]
* [[Wikipedia:Teahouse|The Teahouse]]

I hope you enjoy editing here and being a [[Wikipedia:Wikipedians|Wikipedian]]! Please [[Wikipedia:Signatures|sign]] your messages on [[Help:Using talk pages|talk page]]s using four [[tilde]]s (<nowiki>~~~~</nowiki>); this will automatically insert your username and the date. If you need help, check out [[Wikipedia:Questions]], ask me on [[User talk:Commandant Quacks-a-lot|my talk page]], or ask your question on this page and then place <code><nowiki>{{help me}}</nowiki></code> before the question. Again, welcome! <!-- Template:Welcome-auto --> [[User:Commandant Quacks-a-lot|Commandant Quacks-a-lot]] ([[User talk:Commandant Quacks-a-lot|talk]]) 14:16, 10 June 2026 (UTC)

entry.title: Bobby Fuller
entry.summary:
Death

← Previous revision Revision as of 14:16, 10 June 2026
Line 42: Line 42:


His death was also explored in the May 11, 2015, episode of the [[NPR]] program ''All Things Considered.''<ref>{{cite web|author=Alex Cohen|url=https://www.npr.org/2015/05/11/405955609/new-book-explores-bobby-fullers-mysterious-death|title=New Book Explores Bobby Fuller's Mysterious Death|publisher=NPR.org|date=2015-05-11|access-date=2016-07-19}}</ref> The program references the book ''I Fought the Law: The Life and Strange Death of Bobby Fuller'' by [[Miriam Linna]], with contributions by Randy Fuller.<ref>{{cite book|title=I Fought The Law: The Life and Strange Death of Bobby Fuller|author1=Miriam Linna|author2=Randell Fuller|date=February 2, 2015|publisher=Kicks Books |isbn=978-1-940157-11-5}}</ref> Sometime after the ''Unsolved Mysteries'' segment in question initially aired, the cause of Fuller's death was officially changed from "suicide" to "accident".<ref name="unsolved"/>
His death was also explored in the May 11, 2015, episode of the [[NPR]] program ''All Things Considered.''<ref>{{cite web|author=Alex Cohen|url=https://www.npr.org/2015/05/11/405955609/new-book-explores-bobby-fullers-mysterious-death|title=New Book Explores Bobby Fuller's Mysterious Death|publisher=NPR.org|date=2015-05-11|access-date=2016-07-19}}</ref> The program references the book ''I Fought the Law: The Life and Strange Death of Bobby Fuller'' by [[Miriam Linna]], with contributions by Randy Fuller.<ref>{{cite book|title=I Fought The Law: The Life and Strange Death of Bobby Fuller|author1=Miriam Linna|author2=Randell Fuller|date=February 2, 2015|publisher=Kicks Books |isbn=978-1-940157-11-5}}</ref> Sometime after the ''Unsolved Mysteries'' segment in question initially aired, the cause of Fuller's death was officially changed from "suicide" to "accident".<ref name="unsolved"/>

In November 2008, the Los Angeles County Department of Coroner conducted a formal case review of the 1966 death of Robert Gaston Fuller at the request of Dr. Lakshmanan. The review was triggered by a letter from Neville Johnson, an attorney representing the decedent's heir, who requested that Fuller's body be exhumed and re-examined. The subsequent case review, authored by Chief of the Forensic Medicine Division Dr. Christopher Rogers, re-evaluated the original autopsy findings. Dr. Rogers noted that while the initial death was closed as asphyxia due to gasoline inhalation, the original description of numerous petechial hemorrhages "appears excessive" and could not be fully explained as mere positioning artifacts. The report concluded that the original cause of death was largely based on circumstantial surroundings rather than definitive forensic testing, stating, "I am not certain that asphyxia from causes other than gasoline has been ruled out". Dr. Rogers recommended obtaining the original police records, scene photographs, and suicide team notes for further verification.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://wikimedia.org |title=Los Angeles County Department of Coroner Forensic Consultant's Report: Case Review (Case No. 1966-07379) |author=Dr. Christopher Rogers |date=December 29, 2008 |publisher=County of Los Angeles Department of Coroner |note=Released via Public Records Request on September 26, 2020}}</ref>

:Regarding WaterGen date: James J. Reidy filed his core breakthrough patents for portable atmospheric water generators (AWGs) concurrently on January 30, 1991. [[https://patents.google.com/patent/WO2015054435A1/da 1], [https://patents.google.com/patent/US20160333553A1/en 2]]
:The timeline of his key United States patents and an important subsequent international application spans over a decade: [[https://patents.google.com/patent/WO2015054435A1/da 1], [https://patents.google.com/patent/US20160333553A1/en 2], [https://patents.google.com/patent/AU2017382848B2/en 3]]
:United States Patents
:* US 5,106,512 (Portable Air-Water Generator)
:** Filing Date: January 30, 1991
:** Issue Date: April 21, 1992 [[https://patents.google.com/patent/WO2015054435A1/da 1], [https://patents.google.com/patent/US20160333553A1/en 2]]
:* US 5,149,446 (Potable Water Generator)
:** Filing Date: January 30, 1991

Line 204: Line 204:


=='Shakti Scheme' free bus service for women==
=='Shakti Scheme' free bus service for women==
'Shakti Scheme' was announced by the [[Second Siddaramaiah ministry]] on 2 June 2023. It started on 11 June 2023, providing free-of-charge bus service to Karnataka domicile women. Beneficiaries show their government-issued photo identity and address proof for the first three months. Bus conductors issue them zero-fare tickets Thereafter, beneficiaries obtain Shakti smartcards (named after the name of the scheme) through an application process on the government's [https://sevasindhugs.karnataka.gov.in/ Seva Sindhu website] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231002023018/http://sevasindhugs.karnataka.gov.in/ |date=2 October 2023 }}.<ref>{{Cite web |date=5 June 2023 |title=Guidelines released for free travel bus scheme for women in Karnataka |url=https://www.deccanherald.com/state/top-karnataka-stories/guidelines-released-for-free-travel-bus-scheme-for-women-in-karnataka-1225093.html |access-date=5 June 2023 |website=Deccan Herald |language=en}}</ref>
'Shakti Scheme' was announced by the [[Second Siddaramaiah ministry]] on 2 June 2023. It started on 11 June 2023, providing free-of-charge bus service to Karnataka domicile women. Beneficiaries show their government-issued photo identity and address proof for the first three months. Bus conductors issue them zero-fare tickets Thereafter, beneficiaries obtain Shakti smartcards (named after the name of the scheme) through an application process on the government's Seva Sindhu website {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231002023018/http://sevasindhugs.karnataka.gov.in/ |date=2 October 2023 }}.<ref>{{Cite web |date=5 June 2023 |title=Guidelines released for free travel bus scheme for women in Karnataka |url=https://www.deccanherald.com/state/top-karnataka-stories/guidelines-released-for-free-travel-bus-scheme-for-women-in-karnataka-1225093.html |access-date=5 June 2023 |website=Deccan Herald |language=en}}</ref>

Line 206: Line 206:
Wood is used in the construction of buildings, bridges, trackways, piles, poles for power lines, masts for boats, pit props, railway sleepers, fencing, hurdles, shuttering for concrete, pipes, scaffolding and pallets. In housebuilding it is used in joinery, for making joists, roof trusses, roofing shingles, thatching, staircases, doors, window frames, floor boards, parquet flooring, panelling and cladding.<ref name=Appalachian>{{cite web |url=http://www.appalachianwood.org/utilization.htm |title=Wood utilization |publisher=Appalachian Hardwood Manufacturers |access-date=27 July 2012 |archive-date=28 July 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120728010824/http://www.appalachianwood.org/utilization.htm |url-status=live }}</ref>
Wood is used in the construction of buildings, bridges, trackways, piles, poles for power lines, masts for boats, pit props, railway sleepers, fencing, hurdles, shuttering for concrete, pipes, scaffolding and pallets. In housebuilding it is used in joinery, for making joists, roof trusses, roofing shingles, thatching, staircases, doors, window frames, floor boards, parquet flooring, panelling and cladding.<ref name=Appalachian>{{cite web |url=http://www.appalachianwood.org/utilization.htm |title=Wood utilization |publisher=Appalachian Hardwood Manufacturers |access-date=27 July 2012 |archive-date=28 July 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120728010824/http://www.appalachianwood.org/utilization.htm |url-status=live }}</ref>


[[File:Claude Monet, Weeping Willow.JPG|thumb|upright|Trees in art: ''[[Weeping Willow (painting)|Weeping Willow]]'', [[Claude Monet]], 1918]]
[[File:Claude Monet, Weeping Willow.JPG|thumb|upright|Trees in art: ''[[Weeping Willow (Monet)|Weeping Willow]]'', [[Claude Monet]], 1918]]


Wood is used to construct carts, farm implements, boats, dugout canoes and in shipbuilding. It is used for making furniture, tool handles, boxes, ladders, musical instruments, bows, weapons, matches, clothes pegs, brooms, shoes, baskets, turnery, carving, toys, pencils, rollers, cogs, wooden screws, barrels, coffins, skittles, veneers, artificial limbs, oars, skis, wooden spoons, sports equipment and wooden balls.<ref name=Appalachian/>
Wood is used to construct carts, farm implements, boats, dugout canoes and in shipbuilding. It is used for making furniture, tool handles, boxes, ladders, musical instruments, bows, weapons, matches, clothes pegs, brooms, shoes, baskets, turnery, carving, toys, pencils, rollers, cogs, wooden screws, barrels, coffins, skittles, veneers, artificial limbs, oars, skis, wooden spoons, sports equipment and wooden balls.<ref name=Appalachian/>

entry.title: Espírito Santo do Turvo
entry.summary:
Economy

← Previous revision Revision as of 14:16, 10 June 2026
Line 138: Line 138:
==Economy==
==Economy==
Its [[economy]] is based on [[agriculture]] with large production of [[peanut]]s and [[cotton]]. Espírito Santo do Turvo is an important junction to the south, [[Mato Grosso]], and other regions.
Its [[economy]] is based on [[agriculture]] with large production of [[peanut]]s and [[cotton]]. Espírito Santo do Turvo is an important junction to the south, [[Mato Grosso]], and other regions.

Almost every month some members of the Association of Women of Espírito Santo do Turvo journey more than 300 kilometers to the São Paulo State capital to exhibit and sell their artisan work. Their proceeds combined with a donation from the Levy Straus Foundation is being used to fund their headquarters. <ref>{{Cite web |title=Environmental education against poverty |url=https://revistapesquisa.fapesp.br/en/environmental-education-against-poverty/ |access-date=2026-06-10 |website=revistapesquisa.fapesp.br |language=en-US}}</ref>

entry.title: 1995 CIA disinformation controversy
entry.summary:
Background

← Previous revision Revision as of 14:16, 10 June 2026
Line 13: Line 13:
[[File:EdwardleehowardWantedPoster.jpg|thumb|left|[[FBI]] wanted poster of U.S. defector [[Edward Lee Howard]].]]
[[File:EdwardleehowardWantedPoster.jpg|thumb|left|[[FBI]] wanted poster of U.S. defector [[Edward Lee Howard]].]]


Moreover, by compromising CIA methods, Ames enabled the KGB and its successor, the [[Foreign Intelligence Service (Russia)|SVR]], to filter feed material to the CIA from 1986 to 1993. In 1987, KGB agent Aleksandr Zhomov offered to sell the CIA intelligence on how the Soviets had detected and arrested the CIA's agents. The SE Division accepted Zhomov and assigned him the [[CIA cryptonym|cryptonym]] GTPROLOGUE. Zhomov, who was actually a Soviet double agent assigned to protect Ames, furnished fake documents ascribing the CIA's agent losses to superior KGB surveillance and luck. He then told the CIA that he would identify upcoming KGB plants, but only on the condition that the CIA treat them as genuine, lest the KGB discover the leak and arrest him. The fake agents soon overwhelmed Moscow Station's resources, and Zhomov disappeared in July 1990 after receiving payment from the U.S.{{sfn|Fischer|2016|pp=51–52}}{{sfn|Fischer|2011|p=286}} The spurious sources and the disinformation they provided reportedly convinced the CIA that the 1985–1986 debacle was an anomaly, thus protecting Ames.<ref name="Bogus News" /> Other genuine CIA agents were compromised by the Soviets, who used them to feed false intelligence back to the U.S.<ref name="Conduit" />
Moreover, by compromising CIA methods, Ames enabled the KGB and its successor, the [[Foreign Intelligence Service (Russia)|SVR]], to filter feed material to the CIA from 1986 to 1993. In 1987, KGB agent [[Aleksandr Zhomov]] offered to sell the CIA intelligence on how the Soviets had detected and arrested the CIA's agents. The SE Division accepted Zhomov and assigned him the [[CIA cryptonym|cryptonym]] GTPROLOGUE. Zhomov, who was actually a Soviet double agent assigned to protect Ames, furnished fake documents ascribing the CIA's agent losses to superior KGB surveillance and luck. He then told the CIA that he would identify upcoming KGB plants, but only on the condition that the CIA treat them as genuine, lest the KGB discover the leak and arrest him. The fake agents soon overwhelmed Moscow Station's resources, and Zhomov disappeared in July 1990 after receiving payment from the U.S.{{sfn|Fischer|2016|pp=51–52}}{{sfn|Fischer|2011|p=286}} The spurious sources and the disinformation they provided reportedly convinced the CIA that the 1985–1986 debacle was an anomaly, thus protecting Ames.<ref name="Bogus News" /> Other genuine CIA agents were compromised by the Soviets, who used them to feed false intelligence back to the U.S.<ref name="Conduit" />


The CIA began to investigate the disappearances of its agents, initially suspecting that bad tradecraft, a Soviet breach of Moscow Station or the CIA's communications, or a mole was responsible for the disaster.{{sfn|Hitz|1994}} Suspicions fell on [[Nitrophenyl pentadienal|spy dust]] (METKA), a KGB compromise of CIA cables, or new Soviet surveillance tactics, but these probes proved unsuccessful.{{sfn|Fischer|2011|pp=274–278}} Ames' conspicuous spending drew the attention of the molehunters in late 1989, but he passed a polygraph test. However, the molehunt regained momentum in 1991 as it received more officers to work on the case. Attention focused on Ames and other suspects, and his finances were investigated once more in 1992; in August, the investigators linked Ames' bank deposits with his meetings with the Soviets.{{sfn|Hitz|1994}} In March 1993, the molehunter unit concluded that there was a KGB mole within the CIA who had followed Howard, and the FBI began close surveillance of Ames, monitoring his office and home, and tracking his car.{{sfn|U.S. Senate Select Committee on Intelligence|1994|pp=50-51}} A June 25 search of his office found about 144 classified documents unrelated to his work; an analysis of his finances found an unexplained $1,326,310; and subsequent searches of Ames' trash and residence found further documents on CIA operations and agents, as well as Ames' own espionage for the KGB. On February 21, 1994, Ames and his wife, Rosario, were arrested by FBI agents: The duo pled guilty to espionage, and the [[United States Senate Select Committee on Intelligence|Senate Intelligence Committee]] requested that the CIA's Inspector General review the case.{{sfn|U.S. Senate Select Committee on Intelligence|1994|pp=iii, 51-52}}
The CIA began to investigate the disappearances of its agents, initially suspecting that bad tradecraft, a Soviet breach of Moscow Station or the CIA's communications, or a mole was responsible for the disaster.{{sfn|Hitz|1994}} Suspicions fell on [[Nitrophenyl pentadienal|spy dust]] (METKA), a KGB compromise of CIA cables, or new Soviet surveillance tactics, but these probes proved unsuccessful.{{sfn|Fischer|2011|pp=274–278}} Ames' conspicuous spending drew the attention of the molehunters in late 1989, but he passed a polygraph test. However, the molehunt regained momentum in 1991 as it received more officers to work on the case. Attention focused on Ames and other suspects, and his finances were investigated once more in 1992; in August, the investigators linked Ames' bank deposits with his meetings with the Soviets.{{sfn|Hitz|1994}} In March 1993, the molehunter unit concluded that there was a KGB mole within the CIA who had followed Howard, and the FBI began close surveillance of Ames, monitoring his office and home, and tracking his car.{{sfn|U.S. Senate Select Committee on Intelligence|1994|pp=50-51}} A June 25 search of his office found about 144 classified documents unrelated to his work; an analysis of his finances found an unexplained $1,326,310; and subsequent searches of Ames' trash and residence found further documents on CIA operations and agents, as well as Ames' own espionage for the KGB. On February 21, 1994, Ames and his wife, Rosario, were arrested by FBI agents: The duo pled guilty to espionage, and the [[United States Senate Select Committee on Intelligence|Senate Intelligence Committee]] requested that the CIA's Inspector General review the case.{{sfn|U.S. Senate Select Committee on Intelligence|1994|pp=iii, 51-52}}

Line 31: Line 30:


==Early life==
==Early life==
David Messina was born in 1974 in Rome Italy. He graduated from the Scuola Internazionale di Comics (International School of Comics) in Rome.<ref>[http://www.scuolacomics.com/docenti/cv/david-messina "Master in Colorazione Digitale, Corso di Fumetto"]. Scuola Internazionale di Comics. Retrieved June 29, 2014.</ref>
David Messina was born in 1974 in [[Rome]]. He graduated from the Scuola Internazionale di Comics in Rome.<ref>[http://www.scuolacomics.com/docenti/cv/david-messina "Master in Colorazione Digitale, Corso di Fumetto"]. Scuola Internazionale di Comics. Retrieved June 29, 2014.</ref>


==Career==
==Career==
David Messina did [[storyboarding]] and character design in the animation industry and also worked in [[graphic design]] before breaking into comics.<ref name=OfficialBlog>[https://www.davidmessinart.blogspot.com/ "SPIDER BEHIND THE MIRROR"] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190527164641/http://davidmessinart.blogspot.com/ |date=2019-05-27 }}. [[Blogger (service)|Blogger]]. Retrieved June 29, 2014.</ref>
David Messina did [[storyboarding]] and character design in the animation industry and also worked in [[graphic design]] before moving into comics.<ref name=OfficialBlog>[https://www.davidmessinart.blogspot.com/ "SPIDER BEHIND THE MIRROR"] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190527164641/http://davidmessinart.blogspot.com/ |date=2019-05-27 }}. [[Blogger (service)|Blogger]]. Retrieved June 29, 2014.</ref>
He has published erotic comic books for the Spanish, Italian and French markets. He broke into the American comics industry in 2005.<ref name=SCdC>[http://www.schoolofcomics.us/en/chicago/item/42-david-messina/42-david-messina "David Messina"] {{Webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20140702020012/http://www.schoolofcomics.us/en/chicago/item/42-david-messina/42-david-messina |date=2014-07-02 }}. Scuola Internazionale di Comics. Retrieved June 29, 2014.</ref> He has done work for [[IDW Publishing]], [[Marvel Comics]], [[Image Comics]], [[Devil's Due Publishing]] and [[DC Comics]].<ref name=OfficialBlog/>
He has published erotic comic books for the Spanish, Italian and French markets. He moved into the American comics industry in 2005.<ref name=SCdC>[http://www.schoolofcomics.us/en/chicago/item/42-david-messina/42-david-messina "David Messina"] {{Webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20140702020012/http://www.schoolofcomics.us/en/chicago/item/42-david-messina/42-david-messina |date=2014-07-02 }}. Scuola Internazionale di Comics. Retrieved June 29, 2014.</ref> He has worked for [[IDW Publishing]], [[Marvel Comics]], [[Image Comics]], [[Devil's Due Publishing]] and [[DC Comics]].<ref name=OfficialBlog/>


Since 2002 Messina has taught at his alma mater, the Scuola Internazionale di Comics in Rome, Italy.<ref name=OfficialBlog/><ref name=SCdC/>
Since 2002 Messina has taught at his alma mater, the Scuola Internazionale di Comics.<ref name=OfficialBlog/><ref name=SCdC/>

Bathan is a village in Amargarh Tehsil of Malerkotla district in the Indian state of Punjab. It falls under the PIN Code 148022 and is served by Bathan Branch Post Office. The village is administered by Bathan Gram Panchayat under the Panchayati Raj system.

The '''Central State Film Archive''' ({{langx|sq|Arkivi Qendror Shtetëror i Filmit}}) is the main [[film archive]] of [[Albania]] based in [[Tirana]] which lists in its repository 271 feature films, 166 animated films, 1,131 documentaries, and 1,012 film chronicles between the years 1945–2015.<ref>[http://m.gazetaexpress.com/mobile/arte/elvira-diamanti-restaurimi-i-filmit-shqiptar-i-pamundur-pa-bashkepunimin-me-kosoven-6688/?archive=1 Elvira Diamanti: Restaurimi i filmit shqiptar, i pamundur pa bashkëpunimin me Kosovën]{{Dead link|date=July 2020 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref><ref>[http://sot.com.al/kultura/arkivi-i-filmit-cikël-dokumentarësh-për-jetën-dhe-karrierën-artistike-të-ikonave-të-filmit Arkivi i filmit, cikël dokumentarësh për jetën dhe karrierën artistike të ikonave të filmit shqiptar]</ref>
The '''Central State Film Archive''' ({{langx|sq|Arkivi Qendror Shtetëror i Filmit}}) is the main [[film archive]] of [[Albania]] based in [[Tirana]] which lists in its repository 271 feature films, 166 animated films, 1,131 documentaries, and 1,012 film chronicles between the years 1945–2015.<ref>[http://m.gazetaexpress.com/mobile/arte/elvira-diamanti-restaurimi-i-filmit-shqiptar-i-pamundur-pa-bashkepunimin-me-kosoven-6688/?archive=1 Elvira Diamanti: Restaurimi i filmit shqiptar, i pamundur pa bashkëpunimin me Kosovën]{{Dead link|date=July 2020 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref><ref>[http://sot.com.al/kultura/arkivi-i-filmit-cikël-dokumentarësh-për-jetën-dhe-karrierën-artistike-të-ikonave-të-filmit Arkivi i filmit, cikël dokumentarësh për jetën dhe karrierën artistike të ikonave të filmit shqiptar]</ref>

entry.title: Yukiko Okada
entry.summary:
Legacy

← Previous revision Revision as of 14:16, 10 June 2026
Line 56: Line 56:
[[File:Túmulo de Yukiko Okada.jpg|thumb|Yukiko Okada's grave at the Jōman-ji Temple<ref name="minamino" />]]
[[File:Túmulo de Yukiko Okada.jpg|thumb|Yukiko Okada's grave at the Jōman-ji Temple<ref name="minamino" />]]


Fans who considered her to be [[Seiko Matsuda]]'s successor were shocked and shattered by her untimely death. It resulted in many [[copycat suicide]]s in Japan,<ref name = "greenless" /> soon christened with the [[neologism]] "Yukiko Syndrome"<ref name = "greenless" /> or "Yukko Syndrome."<ref name="googlebooks">[https://books.google.com/books?id=cTLSH4RIqk0C&dq=%22yukiko+syndrome%22&pg=PA97 Japanese Society Since 1945] by Edward R. Beauchamp, Taylor & Francis, 1998, {{ISBN|0-8153-2732-3}}, trang 97</ref><ref name="wetherall" /> By April 26, 1986, 23 out of 36 youth suicides since Okada died were committed by also jumping off a building.<ref>{{cite web |date=26 April 1986 |title=High School Girl Found Burned to Death in Apparent Suicide |url=https://apnews.com/article/3d1740e0935eaeb0c814b050b212f136 |access-date=25 September 2021 |publisher=[[Associated Press]]}}{{dead link|date=April 2024}}</ref>
Fans who expected her to be [[Seiko Matsuda]]'s rightful successor were shocked and shattered by her untimely death. It resulted in many [[copycat suicide]]s in Japan,<ref name = "greenless" /> soon christened with the [[neologism]] "Yukiko Syndrome"<ref name = "greenless" /> or "Yukko Syndrome."<ref name="googlebooks">[https://books.google.com/books?id=cTLSH4RIqk0C&dq=%22yukiko+syndrome%22&pg=PA97 Japanese Society Since 1945] by Edward R. Beauchamp, Taylor & Francis, 1998, {{ISBN|0-8153-2732-3}}, trang 97</ref><ref name="wetherall" /> By April 26, 1986, 23 out of 36 youth suicides since Okada died were committed by also jumping off a building.<ref>{{cite web |date=26 April 1986 |title=High School Girl Found Burned to Death in Apparent Suicide |url=https://apnews.com/article/3d1740e0935eaeb0c814b050b212f136 |access-date=25 September 2021 |publisher=[[Associated Press]]}}{{dead link|date=April 2024}}</ref>


In turn, it has been suggested that Okada may have had in mind idol [[Yasuko Endō]], who also committed suicide by falling from a rooftop ten days earlier.<ref name="googlebooks" /><ref name="wetherall" />
In turn, it has been suggested that Okada may have had in mind idol [[Yasuko Endō]], who also committed suicide by falling from a rooftop ten days earlier.<ref name="googlebooks" /><ref name="wetherall" />

entry.title: Rain on Me (Lady Gaga and Ariana Grande song)
entry.summary:
Remixes: alt

← Previous revision Revision as of 14:16, 10 June 2026
Line 200: Line 200:


==Remixes==
==Remixes==
[[File:Arca_Sonar_1_(cropped).jpg|upright=0.6|thumb|[[Arca (musician)|Arca]] remixed the song for Gaga's [[remix album]], ''[[Dawn of Chromatica]]''.]]
[[File:Arca_Sonar_1_(cropped).jpg|upright=0.6|thumb|[[Arca (musician)|Arca]] remixed the song for Gaga's [[remix album]], ''[[Dawn of Chromatica]]''|alt=Arca sings into a handheld microphone while wearing an in-ear monitor and a black leather harness around her neck and shoulders.
]]
On July 17, 2021, remixes of "Rain on Me" done by [[Ralphi Rosario]] and [[Purple Disco Machine]] were released digitally.<ref name="Purple Disco Machine remix"/><ref name="Ralphi Rosario remix"/> According to an article by the Official Charts Company, the Purple Disco Machine remix arrived amid a surge of high-profile reworks that accompanied the disco revival, helping to solidify his mainstream breakthrough while reworking pop hits into more club-oriented yet faithful versions of the originals.<ref>{{cite web|last=Copsey|first=Rob|date=December 2, 2020|title=Purple Disco Machine talks Hypnotized, the disco revival and remixing Lady Gaga and Dua Lipa|url=https://www.officialcharts.com/chart-news/purple-disco-machine-talks-hypnotized-the-disco-revival-and-remixing-lady-gaga-and-dua-lipa__31771/|access-date=March 10, 2026|publisher=[[Official Charts Company]]|url-status=live|archive-date=March 10, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260310204026/https://www.officialcharts.com/chart-news/purple-disco-machine-talks-hypnotized-the-disco-revival-and-remixing-lady-gaga-and-dua-lipa__31771/}}</ref> Purple Disco Machine's version incorporates funk elements, while Rosario's remix maintains the house-driven rhythm of the original track. Both producers issued two versions of their remixes—a longer club-oriented mix and a shorter edit.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Kent-Smith|first=Jasmine|date=August 10, 2021|title=Lanzan nuevas versiones de "Rain on me", de Lady Gaga y Ariana Grande|language=es|trans-title=New versions of "Rain on Me" by Lady Gaga and Ariana Grande are released|url=https://www.lacapitalmdp.com/lanzan-nuevas-versiones-de-rain-on-me-de-lady-gaga-y-ariana-grande/|url-status=live|access-date=March 10, 2026|website=[[La Capital (Mar del Plata)|La Capital]]|archive-date=March 10, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260310204002/https://www.lacapitalmdp.com/lanzan-nuevas-versiones-de-rain-on-me-de-lady-gaga-y-ariana-grande/}}</ref>
On July 17, 2021, remixes of "Rain on Me" done by [[Ralphi Rosario]] and [[Purple Disco Machine]] were released digitally.<ref name="Purple Disco Machine remix"/><ref name="Ralphi Rosario remix"/> According to an article by the Official Charts Company, the Purple Disco Machine remix arrived amid a surge of high-profile reworks that accompanied the disco revival, helping to solidify his mainstream breakthrough while reworking pop hits into more club-oriented yet faithful versions of the originals.<ref>{{cite web|last=Copsey|first=Rob|date=December 2, 2020|title=Purple Disco Machine talks Hypnotized, the disco revival and remixing Lady Gaga and Dua Lipa|url=https://www.officialcharts.com/chart-news/purple-disco-machine-talks-hypnotized-the-disco-revival-and-remixing-lady-gaga-and-dua-lipa__31771/|access-date=March 10, 2026|publisher=[[Official Charts Company]]|url-status=live|archive-date=March 10, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260310204026/https://www.officialcharts.com/chart-news/purple-disco-machine-talks-hypnotized-the-disco-revival-and-remixing-lady-gaga-and-dua-lipa__31771/}}</ref> Purple Disco Machine's version incorporates funk elements, while Rosario's remix maintains the house-driven rhythm of the original track. Both producers issued two versions of their remixes—a longer club-oriented mix and a shorter edit.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Kent-Smith|first=Jasmine|date=August 10, 2021|title=Lanzan nuevas versiones de "Rain on me", de Lady Gaga y Ariana Grande|language=es|trans-title=New versions of "Rain on Me" by Lady Gaga and Ariana Grande are released|url=https://www.lacapitalmdp.com/lanzan-nuevas-versiones-de-rain-on-me-de-lady-gaga-y-ariana-grande/|url-status=live|access-date=March 10, 2026|website=[[La Capital (Mar del Plata)|La Capital]]|archive-date=March 10, 2026|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260310204002/https://www.lacapitalmdp.com/lanzan-nuevas-versiones-de-rain-on-me-de-lady-gaga-y-ariana-grande/}}</ref>
